title: West Bengal Assembly polls Phase 2 LIVE: Clashes erupt in Nadia; Congress alleges intimidation by TMC
content: The polling booth is set up at Hatra Bazaar Primary School, where tensions reportedly flared between workers of the All India Trinamool Congress (AITC), Indian Secular Front (ISF), and BJP

title: BJP will form government in Bengal with thumping majority: BJP chief Nabin
content: “The BJP will form the government in Bengal. We are winning the elections with a massive majority,” Nitin Nabin said

title: Andhra Pradesh SSC results to be declared on April 30 at 11 a.m., says Lokesh
content: Minister Nara Lokesh says students can check results on the official portal, the Mana Mitra WhatsApp service, the LEAP app and DigiLocker; headmasters can download school-wide results through their logins

title: Congress accuses Rajnath Singh of giving 'clean chit to Pakistan', dubs his remarks at SCO 'anti-national'
content: Jairam Ramesh also alleged that this "new stance" in relation to Pakistan is all part of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's policy of "appeasement" of the U.S.

title: PM Modi holds 14-km roadshow in Varanasi, offers prayers at Kashi Vishwanath temple
content: Devotees and supporters were seen raising slogans such as "Har Har Mahadev" and "Jai Shri Ram" to welcome the Prime Minister

title: Tamil Nadu government cannot restrict maternity leave to just 12 weeks for third pregnancy: Madras High Court
content: Justices R. Suresh Kumar and N. Senthil Kumar say the pain undergone by women employees for all pregnancies are the same and hence they are entitled to 365 days of maternity leave for third pregnancy too

title: Free health camp in Jambagi village in Vijayapura district by B. M. Patil Medical College Hospital and Research Centre
content: The health camp was held in the Government Higher Primary School in Jambagi H. village of Babaleshwar taluk

title: Mumbai to face 10% water cut from May 15
content: “It is to ensure that Mumbai’s water supply remains in good condition, considering the El Niño effect,” BMC Commissioner Ashwini Bhide said

title: Security forces, Maoists exchange fire in Jharkhand's West Singhbhum
content: Misir Besra — a politburo member of CPI (Maoist) — remains active in the area along with his group, according to police sources

title: Anant Ambani urges Colombia to halt culling of Escobar’s hippos, offers to bring all 80 of them to Vantara
content: Pablo Escobar brought the hippos from Africa in the 1980s, but after his death, the hippos from his private zoo made a new life in the Magdalena River — where they have attacked fishermen, prompting moves to cull them

title: NDPS court in Mumbai criticises NCB over freeze order; allows actor Rhea Chakraborty to operate account
content: Additional Sessions Judge U.C. Deshmukh passed the order while allowing the application filed by Rhea Chakraborty and her mother Sandhya

title: At least four killed, 20 injured as bus, truck collide in Odisha
content: The accident took place on Tuesday (April 28, 2026) night in Chandiposh police station area when the bus was travelling from Rourkela to Mangalpur in Jajpur district

title: Telangana SSC results 2026 on four websites, MeeSeva WhatsApp number
content: Hyderabad Police Commissioner appealed to parents not to burden their children with hopes, to observe their behavior after results

title: Lokesh sworn in as TDP national working president; Naidu hits out at YSRCP, calls for unity in alliance
content: Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu administers oath to son Nara Lokesh and 252 members of the new TDP committees at NTR Bhavan; says new committees give proper representation to women

title: Supreme Court says enforcement of existing laws, not new laws, needed to combat hate speech and crimes
content: The court said hate crimes continue to shed blood because of poor enforcement of existing laws, and not because there are no laws to address the issue and punish the perpetrators

title: Fire breaks out in Ghaziabad high-rise; several residents rescued
content: The blaze erupted on the eleventh floor of the Gaur Green Avenue Society at around 9 a.m., spreading to multiple flats across several floors, a fire department official said

title: Students trying to sell IPL match tickets in black among 24 arrested in Bengaluru
content: The police said that they have recovered as many as 36 tickets from the accused, who are private firm employees and students attempting to sell the tickets at higher prices

title: Hike in milk price necessary, will be implemented: Milma
content: Quantum of increase to be decided after a meeting of Milma board, says K.S. Mani, chairman of Kerala Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation

title: Delhi Excise case: AAP leader Durgesh Pathak says he won’t appear for proceedings
content: Mr. Pathak, like AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia, has written a letter to Delhi High Court Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma saying that he would not appear in person in the matter

title: Gyan Bharatam Mission: 30,996 manuscripts documented in Mysuru district so far
content: The Gyan Bharatam Mission aims to build a national repository of centuries-old handwritten texts and records

title: CITU petitions ILO over ‘suppression of workers’ rights by Centre and Uttar Pradesh governments’
content: ‘Government of India and the Uttar Pradesh Government are in violation of fundamental ILO principles’

title: Mamata accuses BJP of trying to ‘rig’ Bengal elections, alleges central forces acting at its behest
content: Mamata Banerjee claimed that some people from outside the state were trying to create disturbances in the constituency and sought immediate intervention from the Election Commission

title: Madras High Court orders CBI probe into ₹397-crore transformer procurement during Senthilbalaji’s tenure
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan direct the Directorate of Vigilance and Anti Corruption to handover the papers to CBI within two weeks

title: Maharashtra defers action over Marathi rule, to begin training campaign for taxi, auto drivers
content: Transport Minister said while the mandate to speak in Marathi remains in effect, driving licences will not be revoked solely on the grounds of inadequate language proficiency during this period

title: ECI ought to provide voting facilities for all eligible citizens: Kerala HC
content: The ECI contended that many postal ballot applications were either defective or were not received (by the officials).

title: Will AAP MPs face disqualification after joining BJP?
content: Is there a law against defection? What are the exceptions to it and how has the Supreme Court interpreted them? What are experts’ view on seven of AAP’s 10 Rajya Sabha members joining the BJP last week?

title: Trinamool accuses Election Commission’s police observer of serious misconduct
content: Trinamool Congress has accused 2011-batch IPS officer of Uttar Pradesh cadre, also a trained dental surgeon, of intimidating and threatening locals in Falta under West Bengal’s Diamond Harbour constituency.

title: Maharashtra ATS begins probe into Thane knife attack
content: The police say they have recovered handwritten notes allegedly indicating the desire of the accused to join the Islamic State, a banned terrorist organisation

title: EC asks NIA to ensure bombs not used to disrupt Bengal polls on April 29
content: The directive comes after recovery of crude bombs in the poll-bound State and reports of a minor blast

title: Congress leader booked under POCSO for allegedly assaulting minor children in Bengaluru
content: The accused has been identified as Anand Naidu, general secretary of the Bengaluru North District Congress Committee (DCC)

title: Belagavi ZP CEO tells officials to ensure no village suffers water scarcity, sets up helplines
content: He told officials that water should be supplied through tankers every day, or every other day, in villages facing water shortage

title: What you need to know about Karnataka’s Rohith Vemula Bill
content: The revised Bill aims to prevent exclusion and injustice while safeguarding the right to education, and the dignity of students, teaching faculty, and non-teaching staff belonging to scheduled castes and scheduled tribes

title: Reschedule work hours, provide drinking water to workers: Centre’s advisory to States on heat wave
content: According to the statement, the Ministry has urged all States/UTs and their organisations to take immediate preventive and mitigation measures to safeguard workers and labourers from the adverse effects of rising temperatures and heatwave conditions across the country

title: National Family Health Survey-6 to be released shortly
content: NFHS-6 covered 6,79,238 households across all States and Union Territories, and was conducted in 2023-24

title: Clothing reality: TN politicians bid for new appeal
content: The spotless white khadi shirt and dhoti is the trademark of Tamil Nadu politicians. However, a few of them are changing the traditional dress code as they feel comfortable in modern attire and think it takes them closer to the younger generation.

title: From veshtis to hoodies: How Tamil Nadu’s election fashion got a Gen Z makeover
content: Ahead of the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, political style is no longer top-down — from DMK’s Stalin to TVK’s Vijay, leaders, supporters, and fans are fuelling a shift to customised outfits, party merch, and social media-driven fashion

title: TMC procured 'artificial fingers' to facilitate false voting in Bhabanipur: Suvendu Adhikari
content: Mr. Adhikari also claimed that as many as 3,810 booth slips distributed by Booth Level Officers (BLOs) in Bhabanipur were returned undelivered

title: On TCS harassment and conversion case
content: Concerns have been raised about the Tata Consultancy Services sexual exploitation and alleged forcible conversions case that involves nine FIRs, related to complaints of alleged sexual exploitation, rape and forcible conversion at the company’s office in Nashik, Maharashtra, between 2022 and 2026

title: Pariwarvaadi parties not willing to permit Indian women to reach Assembly, Parliament: PM
content: PM vows he will “leave no stone unturned” to ensure that women’s right to reservation in legislative bodies is implemented

title: Harsh sun leaves Kerala’s traditional fishers in distress
content: Searing heat drives fish to deeper, cooler waters, escalating the cost of fishing. Warming waters lead to a surge in population of jellyfish, which tear nets and cause allergies to fishers. The working hours come down, the catch gets spoiled faster, and sun leaves the fish vendors perennially exhausted

title: Women lead new tourism wave in Kerala’s Maravanthuruth
content: Women from Maravanthuruth have launched Kerala’s first all-woman kayaking club, Harithatheeram, under the Responsible Tourism Mission. Led by 63-year-old N.G. Indira, the club aims to boost local tourism by offering kayaking experiences along the Arivalthodu canal, part of the Water Street project.

title: A grief divided: funeral held on the Kishanganga’s banks prompts calls to reopen LoC crossing points
content: Siblings of the dead Kupwara resident, who have lived in POK since 1989, bid farewell to their brother from across the river; LoC crossing points used by such divided families were shut by the Centre in 2019

title: Odisha acts fast after tribal man carries sister’s remains to bank
content: Asked by the bank to submit proof of his sister’s death to withdraw money from her account, the tribal man walked the streets carrying her skeletal remains on his shoulder, a disturbing image that went viral and sparked widespread outrage

title: Odisha tribal produces skeleton of his sister’s body as proof to claim the money in her account
content: Authorities in the Patna block assured that Mr. Munda would be helped in securing a legal heir certificate and other documents required for claiming the money in the account

title: Gujarat backs development model, says CM Patel amid BJP sweep in local body polls
content: BJP has won over half of the total seats in the municipal corporations of Surendranagar, Jamnagar, Porbandar, Karamsad-Anand, Nadiad, Navsari, Vapi, Surat and Morbi.

title: If he is Singham then we are Pushpa: Trinamool candidate on IPS officer Ajay Pal Sharma issuing warnings
content: A poll panel official said Mr. Sharma visited the area after the Election Commission received reports that Mr. Khan's men were allegedly collecting voter identity cards from people in the constituency and threatening them

title: West Bengal goes to the polls in the second phase of Assembly election
content: The 142 seats going to the polls in the second phase are spread across seven districts of south Bengal, including Kolkata; about 3.21 crore electors will determine the fate of 1,448 candidates in the fray

title: Assembly election 2026 highlights: Tribunal clears 1,468 electors to vote in second phase of polls in West Bengal, says official
content: According to the Election Commission, voters whose names appear in the supplementary roll can check details by entering their EPIC number on its website

title: How Tamil Nadu waited for Assembly election results over the decades
content: For the first time in Tamil Nadu’s history, the Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) were deployed in all the 234 Assembly segments in the 2001 Assembly elections, mirroring simultaneous rollouts in Kerala, Puducherry, and West Bengal

title: Union government speaking to Anthropic about concerns over Mythos: top MeitY official
content: The impact of artificial intelligence on cybersecurity has emerged as a “very real threat” and it has come up in “very real terms” with the release of Mythos by Anthropic: MeitY Secretary S. Krishnan

title: 'No vacuum in law to deal with the offence' — What Supreme Court said on hate speeches
content: The Supreme Court ruled that current laws adequately address hate speech, stating punishment is a legislative matter and no vacuum exists. However, it encouraged the government to assess if new legislation is needed due to changing social dynamics.

title: From Riyan Parag to ABD: Cricketers seen vaping on camera
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag is in hot water after visuals of him vaping in the dressing room during an IPL 2026 match went viral. This incident raises serious concerns as vaping is banned in India. It adds to a string of player conduct controversies in the league.  The league faces renewed scrutiny over player behavior and rule enforcement.

title: ‘Merci, Dhanyavaad…’: Google CEO Sundar Pichai sends message to 1 billion users
content: Google Translate marks two decades, evolving from a niche project to a global AI powerhouse used by over a billion people.  Initially supporting few languages, it now boasts nearly 250, facilitating understanding across borders.  The tool has advanced from statistical methods to neural networks and now utilizes Gemini models for more fluid, real-time translations, even preserving tone and cadence.

title: Watch: 108 Ambulance soiled by accident victim, staffer takes money, tells wife to clean it
content: A 108 ambulance staffer in Katni district allegedly extorted money from a critically injured road accident victim's family and forced a female relative to clean the ambulance. The disturbing incident, captured on video, has led to an official inquiry and a notice issued to the staffer for an explanation.

title: 'Same aura': 15-year-old Sooryavanshi compared to Dhoni, Tendulkar
content: At just 15, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has become one of the IPL’s biggest attractions with his fearless batting and powerful strokeplay. Former India pacer Mohit Sharma compared the youngster’s nationwide popularity and “aura” to MS Dhoni, saying he has seen only two players command such admiration. Ex-India cricketer Murali Karthik also hailed Sooryavanshi as a “genuine prodigy”, comparing his rise and fame to that of Sachin Tendulkar.

title: Gujarat local body election: Hindu woman scripts victory in Muslim-majority Godhra ward
content: In a remarkable display of unity, independent candidate Apexa Soni secured a win in Godhra's communally sensitive Ward 7, a locality with minimal Hindu voters. Residents, primarily from the Muslim community, rallied behind Soni, who had consistently addressed local civic issues even after a narrow loss in 2021. Her victory highlights people transcending divisions for a dedicated public servant.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to 'Trump accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: PM Modi inaugurates UP's longest expressway; check travel time, route, speed limit
content: One of the most noticeable benefits of Ganga Expressway will be the sharp reduction in travel time. The trip between Meerut and Prayagraj, which currently takes around 10 to 12 hours, is likely to be cut to approximately 6 to 7 hours.

title: ‘It’s an arduous task': Iyer on growing crisis for bowlers after loss
content: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer lamented bowling execution after their first IPL 2026 loss. Despite a strong 224, the team couldn't contain Rajasthan Royals' late surge, particularly from Ferreira and Dubey. Iyer acknowledged the challenge for bowlers in modern T20s but stressed execution is key. He remains optimistic, calling it a learning experience.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office day 41: Ranveer Singh starrer sails past Rs 1780cr worldwide
content: As theaters continue to buzz with excitement, Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' celebrates a triumphant 40-day run, inching ever closer to matching the legendary 'Baahubali 2's box office achievements. This pulse-pounding espionage thriller has raked in an incredible Rs 1780 crore worldwide, bolstered by robust domestic earnings of Rs 1132.99 crore net. Read the full article to know more in detail.

title: Judge in OpenAI trial to Sam Altman and Elon Musk: Try staying away from social media
content: A US District Judge has cautioned Elon Musk and OpenAI leaders, including Sam Altman, to cease social media attacks amid their ongoing trial. The judge urged them to control public comments that could inflame tensions. Musk had previously criticized OpenAI's leadership on X. Both parties agreed to a fresh start, with the trial examining OpenAI's shift from its non-profit roots.

title: Ghaziabad apartment fire: Massive blaze erupts in Indirapuram, multiple flats gutted
content: A massive fire erupted at Gaur Green Avenue society in Indirapuram on Wednesday morning, gutting an estimated 10 to 12 flats and causing panic among residents. Fire tenders responded swiftly to the blaze, which is suspected to have been triggered by an electrical fault. Dousing operations are underway to prevent further spread.

title: Is Trump getting the full picture? Vance raises concerns over Iran war
content: US Vice President J D Vance is questioning the Pentagon's assessment of the Iran conflict, concerned about undisclosed missile stockpile depletion. He fears this could impact responses to global flashpoints like Taiwan, South Korea, and Europe.  Despite official assurances, some believe the situation is being downplayed, potentially affecting future military readiness.

title: Riyan Parag captured vaping inside dressing room during IPL match
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was filmed vaping in the team's dressing room during a live broadcast, sparking controversy. The act is illegal in India under the Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act, 2019, raising concerns about athlete health and regulatory compliance. This incident follows a previous breach of protocol by the RR manager.

title: The Phase 2 trust vote in West Bengal: Who will win the Matua votes?
content: Electoral analysts estimate that Matua voters influence outcomes in 60 to 65 assembly seats. In the second phase alone, they are a key factor in 21 constituencies. In tightly contested elections, this scale of influence gives the community leverage that political parties cannot ignore. This is evident in recent electoral trends.

title: Lizard in paneer tikka? Bengaluru cabbie hospitalised after bakery visit
content: A man in Bengaluru was hospitalized after allegedly finding a lizard in his paneer tikka roll from a local bakery. He experienced vomiting and diarrhea, leading to a 24-hour observation period. Police have registered a case, though the complainant could not provide photographic evidence of the alleged contamination.

title: IPL 2026: For batters, struggle is real in slightly tough conditions
content: Having Been Spoon-Fed T20 Belters, They Have Been Unable To Adapt When Needed Most. Should Indian Cricket Be Worried About Success Away From Home?

title: Bengal phase 2 polls: TMC worker attacked, BJP claims voter booth vandalised
content: West Bengal's second phase of Assembly elections commenced amidst reports of violence, voter intimidation, and technical issues. Incidents were reported across Chapra, Shantipur, Nimtala, and Bhangar. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee alleged bias from observers and TMC party flags being removed, while the BJP reported an assault on a polling agent in Chapra.

title: Meghalaya 'honeymoon murder' case: Raja Raghuvanshi’s family shocked after Sonam gets bail
content: The family of slain Indore businessman Raja Raghuvanshi is demanding a CBI probe after his wife, a prime accused in his murder, was granted bail by a Meghalaya court. Raja's mutilated body was found in June last year, and his wife, along with others, was arrested.

title: Watch: 6 wickets in 6 balls! Cricketer scripts history with bowling
content: Myles Davis made cricket history by taking six wickets in six balls for Penkridge Cricket Club against Pelsall Cricket Club in the South Staffordshire County League. Davis finished with figures of 7-16 and described the achievement as “surreal” and “amazing”, with the rare double hat-trick believed to be only the seventh such feat worldwide.

title: IPL 2026: Will Rohit Sharma play vs SRH tonight? Big update on MI star
content: Rohit Sharma's availability for Mumbai Indians' crucial match against Sunrisers Hyderabad remains uncertain due to a hamstring injury. Despite batting in the nets, the team management is hesitant to risk him unless fully fit. MI's batting struggles, particularly in the Powerplay, have been amplified by his absence, while SRH boasts a formidable batting lineup and an improving bowling attack.

title: Expecting a summer baby? Ideal colour to keep your baby calm, comfortable in nursery
content: Parents can create a serene summer nursery with a specific paint shade - a soft colour that works with natural light to keep babies calm and comfortable. Complementary shades can be used for accents but the main goal is a bright yet soothing environment for your baby's early development at home.

title: 'Politics has its place': Akhilesh Yadav visits BJP MLA who was injured while burning his effigy
content: SP leader Akhilesh Yadav visited BJP MLA Anupama Jaiswal in a Lucknow hospital after she sustained severe facial burns while attempting to set fire to an effigy. Yadav expressed his wishes for her speedy recovery, emphasizing that political differences should not overshadow compassion and human relationships. Jaiswal's husband lauded the visit as an act of "human sensitivity and democratic courtesy."

title: ‘Fair and Lovely babua’: Mahua Moitra takes swipe at ‘Singham’ IPS officer Ajay Pal Sharma
content: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra shared nightclub videos of an Election Commission observer, a UP-cadre IPS officer, amid a controversy over his alleged intimidation of TMC workers. The officer, deployed for West Bengal polls, had previously warned "troublemakers" of "appropriate treatment."

title: Bengal turns fortress for phase-II poll: 2.3 lakh CAPF and 38,297 state cops in place
content: West Bengal's final assembly polls are underway with an unprecedented security deployment across 142 constituencies. Over 2.3 lakh central forces and state police are on duty, alongside observers and NIA teams, to ensure a fear-free and fair election. A significant crackdown has led to over 800 arrests and preventive detentions, targeting potential troublemakers in sensitive zones.

title: Suicide or foul play? Mystery shrouds Bengaluru techie’s death; body found in locked flat
content: A 34-year-old software engineer, Puja Dutta, was found dead in her Bengaluru home under suspicious circumstances. Police suspect suicide due to the door being locked from the inside and cut marks on her body, though investigations are ongoing. Her parents have been contacted and are en route to the city.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today
content: Stock market recommendations: Jio Financial Services Ltd, Hindustan Zinc, and PVR INOX are the top stock recommendations by Mehul Kothari, DVP - Technical Research at Anand Rathi Shares as the top stock recommendations for April 29, 2026:

title: ‘Lone wolf’ stabbing near Mumbai: Guard’s desperate cries caught on camera
content: A man, identified as Zaib Zubair Ansari, allegedly attacked two security guards at a Mumbai construction site with a sharp weapon. Investigators are probing the incident as a possible "lone wolf" terror attack, with early findings pointing towards radicalization. Digital devices and notes referencing the Islamic State were recovered from his residence.

title: 'Vote in record numbers': PM Modi appeals for high voter turnout in phase-2 of West Bengal polls
content: Prime Minister Modi urged record voter turnout for West Bengal's Phase-2 elections, especially from women and youth. Voting commenced across 142 constituencies, a crucial phase for the TMC's dominance in southern districts against the BJP's challenge. Key contests include Bhabanipur, with extensive security and webcasting in place for 3.21 crore voters.

title: 'Felt defeated so many times': Manu revisits journey amid Sooryavanshi row
content: Two-time Olympic medallist Manu Bhaker celebrated 10 years in shooting with an emotional Instagram post reflecting on her journey, setbacks and achievements. Bhaker recalled overcoming disappointments, including Asian Games near-misses, and revisited her 2018 Commonwealth Games gold. Her post came amid online debate after she was asked about teenage cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi at an NRAI event.

title: 'He’s so cute': Donald Trump says his mother had a 'crush' on King Charles
content: President Donald Trump shared a light-hearted anecdote about his mother's admiration for the British royal family. He mentioned her fondness for Queen Elizabeth II and playfully suggested she had a crush on the young Prince Charles. The comments were made during an official ceremony at the White House to welcome King Charles III and Queen Camilla to the United States.

title: Another day, another record! Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts IPL history
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi achieved a historic feat, becoming the fastest to 400 runs in an IPL season, needing just 167 balls. His explosive 43 off 16 deliveries set the stage for Rajasthan Royals' record chase of 223 against Punjab Kings.  This remarkable performance cements his status as a rising star.

title: Gujarat Tax Official Arrested For Demanding Rs 20 Lakh To Settle GST Probe
content: The case originated when the CGST Superintendent allegedly demanded Rs 25 lakh to settle an investigation regarding unpaid GST

title: BJP Slams Trinamool's 'Diamond Harbour Model', Says Symbol 'Taped' At Booths
content: Amit Malviya, the BJP's media in-charge, has demanded repolling in all affected booths in Falta where such election frauds have occurred.

title: Samsung Dynasty's Wealth Doubles To $45 Billion In Just One Year
content: The Lees' combined wealth climbed to about $45.5 billion as of March, from roughly $20.1 billion a year earlier, according to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index.

title: 6 Lanes, 600 km, 6 Hours: Ganga Expressway Where You Can Drive At 120 kmph
content: Built at Rs 36,230 crore,Ganga Expressway is Uttar Pradesh's longest expressway and one of India's biggest greenfield road projects.

title: China-US Tensions Build Over Iran And AI Before Donald Trump Meets Xi Jinping
content: In the final weeks before the summit, Washington and Beijing are each using the negotiating window to armour their supply chains, lock down critical intellectual property and build leverage.

title: Bengal Elections Phase 2 Voting LIVE: Bengal Records Nearly 40% Voter Turnout Till 11 AM
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Phase 2 Voting LIVE Updates: The second phase of voting for the intense BJP vs Trinamool battle will begin shortly.

title: Sagar Adani Pushes For "Energy Backbone" Anchored In Resources In India
content: New Delhi, April 29 (IANS) Sagar Adani, Executive Director of renewable energy major Adani Green Energy, said on Wednesday that at our core, we see ourselves not just as operators of infrastructure, but as builders of India’s energy backbone for the

title: UAE Exit Blindsides OPEC And Threatens To Shake Its Grip On Oil
content: For OPEC and its partners, the departure will dilute their ability to manage oil prices by adjusting supply, while positioning the UAE as a wild card player at a time of unprecedented upheaval in the global market.

title: 'Amount Settled': Odisha Bank After Man Brings Sister's Skeleton To Branch
content: The bank, in a post on X, said the incident took place as the man, Jitu Munda from Keonjhar's Dianali village, was "unwilling" to accept procedures explained by the branch manager.

title: EVM 'Glitch', Violence During Polling, Mamata Banerjee's "Terrorism" Charge
content: This is the second and final phase of the Bengal election, and it takes place against the backdrop of the controversial EC-mandated Special Intensive Revision, or SIR, of the state's electoral roll.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Did Pak 'Sabotage' Iran's Messages To Trump? The Myth That Is 'Islamic Brotherhood'
content: Just last week, Iran carried out strikes on terror camps inside Pakistan, targeting Jaish-al-Adl, an organisation that has long been operating in the border regions.

title: Tiruchendur To Shirdi: Vijay Goes On Temple Run Ahead Of May 4 Poll Results
content: Vijay's temple visits come as he takes on both the ruling DMK and the opposition AIADMK, both parties with deep organisational strength.

title: Wazed Ali Choudhury: Assam MLA's Big Bet In Newly Formed Birsing-Jarua Seat
content: Wazed Ali Choudhury has a long electoral history in Dhubri as he has represented the Salmara South constituency multiple times.

title: The Saudi-Pakistan Factor Behind UAE's Exit From OPEC
content: The UAE has also been frustrated with Pakistan, partly because of its deepening ties with Saudi Arabia, but also because of what it considers Islamabad's meek role as a mediator between the US and Iran.

title: Exit Polls Today: When And Where To Watch As NDTV Decodes Voter Trends
content: NDTV brings you the latest updates on exit polls after voting concludes in four states and one Union territory today.

title: With No End To Iran War In Sight, Should You Invest In US Stocks?
content: Stock Market Today: Historically, US equities acted as a relative safe haven during global stress. But Iran war has added a new layer of uncertainty.

title: Telangana Couple Goes For Counselling, Leaves Daughters At Police Station
content: Ravi Kumar, a shop owner, and Lalitha, a nursing student, had a love marriage nearly a decade ago. Over the years, their relationship grew strained, resulting in frequent arguments.

title: Karachi Residents Store Cooking Gas In Balloons Amid Fuel Shortage: Report
content: Residents said the balloons, available in local markets for around Rs 1,000 to Rs 1,500, are filled when gas supply is restored for short durations.

title: West Bengal elections: Phase-1 vote surge new poll rider in TMC’s comfort zone
content: the second phase of vote to elect who will govern Bengal for the next five years has got one extra unlikely parameter . the first phase's stupendous voting percentage of more than 93% has turned the vote in what should have been the Trinamool's zone of comfort into a battle of arithmetic and nerves . both parties trying to crystal-gaze into what an unprecedented voter turnout may mean .
